rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RICHARD G. MENDRIBIL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To The Amended Petition To Remove Richard Mendribil, Jr., As Successor Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Richard Mendribil, Jr. To Report And For First And Final Accounting; For Appointment Of New Trustee; And To Transfer The Place Of Administration Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Richard Mendribil, Jr.'s Demurrer to the Amended Petition to Remove Richard Mendribil, Jr. as Successor Trustee for Breach of Trust; to Compel Richard Mendribil, Jr. to Report and for First and Final Accounting; for Appointment of New Trustee; and to Transfer the Principal Place of Administrationof Trust ("Amended Petition") is OVERRULED. Probade Code Sections 17000, 17004 give the Court jurisdiction over the subject matter of the Amended Petition, which raises issues concerning the internal affairs of the Trust and involves property situated in California. Additionally, the Petitioners have stated facts sufficient to state a cause of action under Probate Code Section 17453(c). Because the Maricopa County Court has not asserted jurisdiction over the Trust, Petitioners' statements in paragraph 113 of the Amended Petition are true and complete: "no other court has assumed jurisdiction over the administration of the trust, and . . . no approval of any other jurisdiction is necessary before this Court orders the transfer of the administration of trust from Arizona to California." The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than February 10, 2016, at 4:00 p.m.
